name: Signed Script Proxy Execution
description: Detects the use of signed Windows binaries like `cscript.exe`, `wscript.exe`, `certutil.exe`, and `jjs.exe` to execute scripts. Adversaries may abuse these legitimate tools for proxy execution of malicious code, which is a common defense evasion technique.

prompts.prompt: An alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a signed Windows binary, **{image}**, was executed with a suspicious command line: **{command}**. This is a strong indicator of **Signed Script Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1216**), where an adversary is attempting to use a trusted utility to execute malicious code. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious file. The event occurred under the user **{user}**.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the signed binary that was executed.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line that was executed.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
